body_txt: In fiction, crocodilians come in hyperbolic sizes ranging from truck-sized to city-crushing. These crocs can eat humans whole, easily squish any tank or other vehicle coming their way, and capsize cruises simply by flipping them over in the water. Unlike most species size exaggerations , there is Truth in Television to crocodilian size ranges. Certain crocodilians grow from feeding, and the longer they live, the bigger they get (albeit, they can't reach sizes that would let them gulp a human down in one piece). However, truly gigantic crocodiles did exist once upon a time—some primitive crocodilians were Dire Beast creatures of the Perilous Prehistoric Seas and other bodies of water, and they were much larger and much more dangerous than their descendants. Giant crocodilians are also a common motif in maritime mythologies as a type of Sea Monster alongside whales , cephalopods , and sharks . When seafaring snakes serve as stock sea monsters, crocodilian sea monsters tend to be a greater threat than their serpentine cohorts, given that crocodilians are equipped with a more versatile arsenal that can make snakes look cuddly by comparison. Due to their association with water, giant crocodiles are often portrayed as a type of water elemental , as well as mythological creatures such as Kraken and Leviathan . While they were often dangerous and destructive , these tanks of reptiles also had application as guardians of civilizations familiar to them, who partook in a form of Giant Animal Worship . In terms of appearance, these creatures are usually oversized crocodilians that more or less resemble modern ones. In some cases, they will be or resemble prehistoric types such as Deinosuchus or Sarcosuchus . Others can be quite monstrous, with some bearing aquatic elements, such as fins and gills, or traits of other reptiles , such as a shell or a forked tongue. This trope naturally crosses over with Kaiju , and in these scenarios, the monster may act as a Not Zilla . Titan-sized crocodiles may also be presented as primitive types of dragons . This is because, in Medieval and Asian cultures, the term "dragon" was often used for giant reptiles like crocodilians and large lizards. Also, being exotic animals occupying niche areas rarely seen by people meant travelers could exaggerate crocodilians as massive, mystical beasts. Today, the Chinese and Japanese words for "alligator" are used as synonyms for "dragon", and the Hebrew word for "dragon", tannin , is still used for crocodiles and snakes. Given that several crocodilians are already relatively large enough to match the length of humans note with the largest species, the saltwater crocodile, able to reach a maximum of 20 feet with tail , to qualify for this trope, the crocodilian in question must be the same size as or larger than the prehistoric Sarcosuchus (which was the size of a schoolbus) and be big enough to devour humans in one bite. Sub-Trope of Dire Beast and Sea Monster . See also Turtle Island and The Great Serpent for other giant reptiles. Often an application for Slurpasaur . May overlap with Dinosaurs Are Dragons and Croc-Scaled Saurian , due to the close association between crocodiles and dinosaurs in media.
